examples: In 1700, a spinster with a pedal-driven spinning wheel might take 200 hours to produce a pound of yarn.
"Let us see, father," said the old spinster.
Spinster was almost like a professional title similar to baker or tailor.
Truman Capote loosely based his 1951 novella on his childhood with spinster aunts in the South.
Katie's employer, timid spinster Amelia Gregg, is played with spirited delicacy by Margaret Daly.
